service.name: Amazon S3 API
service.description_for_ai: World leading cloud object storage. Store files, images, videos, backups. Highly durable, globally available. 5GB free first year. Use for file uploads, static hosting, backups, media storage.

auth.how_to_get: AWS account at https://aws.amazon.com — 5GB free tier

capabilities.description_for_ai: Upload a file or data to an S3 bucket. Returns ETag confirming upload success. File is immediately available at its URL.

bucket.description: S3 bucket name e.g. 'my-company-assets'. Must exist and you must have write permission.

key.description: Object path/name in bucket e.g. 'images/product-123.jpg' or 'backups/2024-01-15.tar.gz'

body.description: File content as binary stream, string, or bytes object

ContentType.description: MIME type: image/jpeg, image/png, application/pdf, text/html, application/json, video/mp4

ACL.description: Access control: private (default), public-read, public-read-write, authenticated-read

Metadata.description: Custom metadata as key-value pairs: {author: 'John', version: '2.0'}

capabilities.description_for_ai: Download a file from S3 bucket. Returns the object body as a stream.

Range.description: Byte range for partial download: 'bytes=0-1023' for first 1KB

VersionId.description: Specific version ID if versioning enabled

agent_hints.sdk: Use AWS SDK (boto3 for Python, aws-sdk for JS) instead of raw HTTP — handles auth automatically.
agent_hints.presigned: Use presigned URLs for temporary access without exposing credentials.
